completed_tasks.name: Wave 2 — build driver, CI, signing (52 automated tests pass)
Plan 86-05 Task 1 — build + install on rig
completed_tasks.progress: build.sh invoked with SKIP_SIGN=1; BUILD_FAIL before any module built

blockers.description: Flatpak build fails at rofiles-fuse setup: 'fusermount: file descriptor 4 is not a socket, can't send fuse fd' -> 'Failure spawning rofiles-fuse, exit_status: 256' -> BUILD_FAIL reason=builder_failed. Root cause: system flatpak-builder binary is ABSENT, so build.sh:51-54 falls back to 'flatpak run org.flatpak.Builder' (Flathub stable/v0-Flathub). The Flatpak'd builder's nested bubblewrap sandbox cannot pass the FUSE fd to the host fusermount. Known org.flatpak.Builder bug, NOT a manifest defect. Build died before checking out any module, so io.github.kcreasey.MusicStreamer.yaml was never exercised.
blockers.workaround: PREFERRED: sudo apt install flatpak-builder (build.sh:51 already prefers the system binary -> bypasses the nested-sandbox FUSE bug, zero code change). ALT: patch build.sh:68-73 to add --disable-rofiles-fuse (no sudo, but loses read-only source protection and modifies the verified driver).

human_actions_pending.context: System flatpak-builder absent; installing it lets build.sh use the host binary and skip the org.flatpak.Builder rofiles-fuse bug. Needs sudo (interactive).

human_actions_pending.action: After install, in-sandbox UAT (SC1-SC5) requires a human on the Wayland rig: audible audio per AAC tier, interactive GBS.FM login, OS media keys, GNOME Software listing
human_actions_pending.context: Plan 86-05 is the human-gate runbook in 86-VERIFICATION.md; automation cannot cover these.

decisions.decision: Classify the build failure as BLOCKED (environment), not FAILED (defect)
decisions.rationale: flatpak-builder never reached a build module; the failure is in the host toolchain fallback path, not the manifest or build driver. Waves 1-2 (52 tests) remain valid.
build.sh was already written to prefer the system binary; installing it is a zero-code-change fix vs. modifying the verified build driver.

next_action: Have Kyle run 'sudo apt install flatpak-builder', then re-run 'SKIP_SIGN=1 bash tools/linux-flatpak/build.sh' and confirm BUILD_OK + SIGN_SKIPPED before proceeding to SC1-SC5 UAT.
context_notes: We diagnosed the BUILD_FAIL from the /clear scrollback. Confirmed: not in a container (init.scope, no .containerenv), /dev/fuse present world-RW, fusermount present, but system flatpak-builder absent and rofiles-fuse only exists inside the org.flatpak.Builder sandbox. The build driver behaved correctly (tried system binary, fell back as designed) — the fallback target has the bug. OPEN QUESTION raised but not yet decided: should build.sh warn loudly or hard-fail when it falls back to org.flatpak.Builder, so this rig-toolchain gap is caught up front next time instead of mid-build? That's a candidate follow-up fix to the driver, separate from the UAT.
